items.headline: Rob Thomson is not on the hot seat despite the Phillies' slow start, Dave Dombrowski says
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — Dave Dombrowski says he's looking at “everything that’s taking place” with Philadelphia's poor start to the season, and he has no immediate concerns about Rob Thomson being the manager to get the Phillies back to the World Series.
Speaking to reporters ahead of a 7-4 loss to the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday night — Philadelphia's seventh loss in a row — the Phillies' president of baseball operations stood behind Thomson's work and said he's been a good manager since replacing Joe Girardi in 2022.

items.headline: Court confirms Neymar corruption acquittal over Barcelona transfer
items.description: Spain's Supreme Court on Wednesday announced it had confirmed the acquittal of Neymar and former Barcelona presidents of corruption charges centred on the Brazil star's...

items.headline: Veteran who hasn't played for England for 8 years is making a surprise run for the World Cup
items.description:
MANCHESTER, England (AP) — At the age of 35 and in the form of his life, England may have found the unlikely answer to a decade-long problem just in time for the World Cup.
Whether coach Thomas Tuchel takes a chance on a veteran striker who hasn't played for his country for eight years, is a question that's growing ever louder.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Miguel Cabrera of the Tigers becomes the 33rd member of the 3,000 hit club
items.description:
April 23
1903 — The New York Highlanders won their first game as a major league team, 7-2 over the Washington Senators.

items.headline: After Fernando Mendoza at No. 1, the NFL draft’s QB board could go quiet fast
items.description:
Don't expect a run of quarterbacks to go off the board in the first round of this year's NFL draft like what happened in 2021 and 2024.
There figures to be a long gap between Fernando Mendoza's coronation as the expected No. 1 pick by the Las Vegas Raiders and when the second quarterback on most public big boards, Ty Simpson, is expected to go.

items.headline: Giants' Ryan Borucki: Picks up hold Tuesday
items.description: Borucki earned a hold over a scoreless inning in Tuesday's 3-1 win over the Dodgers, allowing one hit while striking out one.

items.headline: Giants' Matt Gage: Earns hold Tuesday
items.description: Gage picked up a hold over two-thirds of a scoreless inning in Tuesday's 3-1 win over the Dodgers, issuing one walk.

items.headline: Brewers' Quinn Priester: Kicking off rehab assignment
items.description: Priester (wrist) will begin a rehab assignment at Triple-A Nashville on Wednesday, Adam McCalvy of MLB.com reports.

items.headline: Browns' Quinshon Judkins: Rehabbing at voluntary minicamp
items.description: Judkins (ankle) was spotted in uniform and rehabbing at the start of Cleveland's voluntary minicamp on Tuesday, Dylan Feltovich of USA Today reports.
